topic_title: Anchoring Bias — Over-Relying on the First Piece of Information Received

soft_findings.description: reading.meta.json uses 'reading_minutes' instead of 'estimated_read_minutes', and 'primary_resources_cited' instead of 'citations_used'. The required data is present and correct; only the field names deviate from the SKILL spec. Does not block pass.

format_discipline.notes: First non-empty line is '# Anchoring Bias...' (no frontmatter). Meta parses successfully. All 6 sections present in correct order.

length_budget.notes: 1340 words at 200 wpm = 6.7 min → 7 min. Both within standard profile budget.

citation_discipline.notes: [1] simplypsychology.org cited at lines 5, 11, 13, 24, 28, 36, 91, 104 in reading. Bibliography entries [1]–[3] all matched. No orphans.

no_forward_references.notes: Reading references only 9.1 (System 1), 9.2 (System 2), 9.3 (confirmation bias) — all prior topics. No mention of Judgment Framework, automation bias, or any topic 9.5+.

sample.assertion: Definition of anchoring bias as over-weighting first information
Tversky & Kahneman wheel-of-fortune experiment: 10/65 anchors, 25%/45% responses, ~20pp shift
Adjustment heuristic: start at anchor, nudge up/down, stop when feels right
System 2 is effortful and stops early (insufficient adjustment)
Anchor activates anchor-consistent information in memory
Anchors shift estimates 10–35% in typical experiments
Worked example: $180k AI estimate → $160k adjusted vs $95k–$110k independent range
First-pass anchor: AI first draft anchors all revision cycles
Experts show anchoring effects — just slightly reduced

beginner_readability.notes: All non-everyday terms (anchor, adjustment heuristic, insufficient adjustment) are bold-defined on first use. Concepts introduced with concrete examples before abstractions. One mild hedging phrase ('Research suggests') — well under the 3-instance threshold.

formatting.notes: No paragraph exceeds 4 sentences. The 4-step adjustment process is a numbered list. Worked Example uses numbered steps. Two comparison tables present. Inline examples appear in Overview and Key Concepts. Bold-term definitions used throughout (Anchor, Adjustment heuristic, Insufficient adjustment, etc.).

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights found.

scope.notes: Reading covers exactly anchoring bias, adjustment heuristic, insufficient adjustment, primacy-effect-as-anchor (brief inline mention, not taught as separate concept). No automation bias. No Judgment Framework. Comparison with confirmation bias (9.3) is within scope as a named-and-contrasted prior concept. All AI-context applications are anchoring-bias applications, not adjacent topics.

escalation_check.result: No escalation — 0 scope_creep findings, 0 combined checks-5-6 findings.

gold_example.notes: No gold example exists for topic 9.4 in .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/. Evaluated against written standards only.

summary: All checks passed. One soft non-blocking finding: reading.meta.json field names deviate from SKILL spec ('reading_minutes' vs 'estimated_read_minutes', 'primary_resources_cited' vs 'citations_used') — data is correct, names differ. Reading is well-structured, beginner-accessible, faithfully derived from corpus, within length budget, and scope-clean.
